schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: \subsection*{1. Minimal criticality condition} Let $\rho_{\rm vac}$ be vacuum energy density with effective pressure $p(\rho_{\rm vac})$. A TZP nucleates when \begin{equation}\label{eq:TZP} \frac{\partial p}{\partial \rho_{\rm vac}}=0,\qquad \Delta \rho_{\rm vac}\to \rho_{\rm matter}+\rho_{\rm field}. \end{equation}
theory.technical_interpretation: Minimal Criticality Condition is a variational / legendre derivative, written labeleq:TZP frac∂ p∂ ρ_rm vac=0, Δ ρ_rm vac→ ρ_rm matter+ρ_rm field.. It arises from a variational or Legendre procedure, defining conjugate quantities or stationarity conditions. It is built from Δ, v, a, c, m, r, defining labeleq:TZP frac∂ p∂ ρ_rm vac. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: Δ, v, a, c, m, r
